name: Arvo Cafe
tagline: Aussie-inspired at SALT Kakaako — the ube bread alone is worth the trip.
parking: SALT has street parking and a lot behind the complex
talkStory: Founded by Australian expat Jess Santelices, who brought Melbourne cafe culture to Kakaako. ‘Arvo’ is Aussie slang for afternoon. The signature ube (purple yam) toast became a viral sensation and put this place on the map — it’s made with real Filipino ube, not food coloring. The flat whites are legit Melbourne-style, pulled with Hawaiian-roasted beans. Get there right at 8am opening on weekdays; the pastry case and that famous ube toast sell out fast.
